2026-05-26.patch_summary: This patch adds documentation for the UFFDIO_RWPROTECT ioctl, which installs or removes read-write protection on a userfaultfd-registered memory range. The ioctl supports two mode bits: UFFDIO_RWPROTECT_MODE_RWP and UFFDIO_RWPROTECT_MODE_DONTWAKE. It also covers the behavior of read-write protection for populated pages only, anonymous vs file-backed reclaim, and error handling. The patch introduces a new man page (UFFDIO_RWPROTECT.2const) to document this ioctl.

reviews.author: Alejandro Colomar
reviews.summary:
Reviewer pointed out that the documentation for UFFDIO_RWPROTECT_MODE_DONTWAKE is unclear and requested clarification on whether this mode bit can be used independently or only in conjunction with UFFDIO_RWPROTECT_MODE_RWP.

Reviewer Alejandro Colomar thanked the submitter for the patch and mentioned that they had applied it after making some minor adjustments.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Kiryl,

On 2026-05-26T14:41:45+0100, Kiryl Shutsemau wrote:

---

Thanks!  I've applied the patch (with a few minor tweaks).


Have a lovely day!
Alex

2026-05-28.patch_summary: This patch adds documentation for the UFFDIO_RWPROTECT ioctl, which installs or removes read-write protection on a userfaultfd-registered memory range. The ioctl supports two mode bits: UFFDIO_RWPROTECT_MODE_RWP and UFFDIO_RWPROTECT_MODE_DONTWAKE. The documentation covers the semantics of these mode bits, as well as error handling and behavior for anonymous and file-backed mappings. The patch creates a new manual page, UFFDIO_RWPROTECT.2const, to document this ioctl.
